items.title: Ex-CEO acquitted of two abuse of power charges in Sabah court
items.summary: Sulaiman had, on Feb 4, 2022, pleaded not guilty to both charges. https://www.nst.com.my/news/crime-courts/2022/02/768706/former-ceo-charged-power-abuse-involving-more-rm2-million On the first charge, Sulaiman, in his capacity as a public officer and CEO of YBS Tenaga Sdn Bhd, was accused of using his position for gratification by appointing Syarikat Pemborong Tambun Jaya to carry out Mechanical and Electrical Works (33KV Transmission Line) valued at RM2,275,115.92. Both charges stated that Sulaiman was a co-owner of Syarikat Pemborong Tambun Jaya, which was a subcontractor for YBS Tenaga Sdn Bhd for the said projects.

items.title: Johor assembly passes bill allowing appointment of non-elected assemblymen
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI: The Johor State Legislative Assembly today passed a bill to amend the state enactment, allowing for the appointment of up to five non-elected state assemblymen to strengthen the composition and functions of the legislative institution. Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, when tabling the bill at Bangunan Sultan Ismail here, said the proposed appointments were aimed at broadening participation in the State Legislative Assembly, especially through the involvement of individuals with expertise and experience to enhance debate and the policymaking process. Onn Hafiz said the bill, among others, included a second clause which seeks to allow the appointment of no more than five non-elected assemblymen.

items.title: Anwar arrives in Cebu for Asean leaders' summit
items.summary: CEBU: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim arrived in Cebu, Philippines, today to attend the 48th Asean Summit and related meetings, which will continue until tomorrow. Also present to welcome Anwar was Malaysia's Ambassador to the Philippines Datuk Abdul Malik Melvin Castelino Anthony. In addition to attending the 48th Asean Summit, Anwar is also scheduled to participate in the Brunei Darussalam–Indonesia–Malaysia–Philippines East Asean Growth Area (BIMP-EAGA) Special Summit this afternoon.

items.title: Meeting on Kampung Bohol flood retention pond set for May 12, says Hannah Yeoh
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A meeting will be held on May 12 to discuss the status of the Kampung Bohol flood retention pond following concerns over flash floods in the area. Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh said the meeting would be convened by Federal Territories Department director-general Datuk Azmi Mohd Zain. She said the discussion would also involve the Kuala Lumpur Land and Mines Office.

items.title: Passengers on hantavirus-stricken ship sway between fear, boredom
items.summary: Instead, the cruise on the hantavirus-stricken MV Hondius was left stranded off Cape Verde with passengers in their cabins, medics in protective suits tending to the sick and the ship's operator seeking a safe port. After four days stationed off the West African archipelago, the ship set sail late Wednesday toward the Canary island of Tenerife, where around 150 remaining passengers and crew may finally disembark under medical supervision. Cruise operator Oceanwide Expeditions has told passengers to follow "isolation measures, hygiene protocols and medical monitoring."

items.title: Emirates reports record 2025/26 group revenue, up 3pct on year
items.summary: DUBAI: Emirates Group posted record group revenue of 150.5 billion dirhams (US$40.98 billion) in the 2025/26 financial year, up 3 per cent from the previous year, it said in a statement. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Zahid: Malaysia mulling ‘filial responsibility’ law to ensure children care for ageing parents
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 7 — The government is considering a new law that would legally obligate children to care for their ageing parents, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said today. Speaking to reporters after launching the National Social Policy Action Plan 2026-2030, Zahid said the proposed legislation aims to introduce a legal framework placing clearer emphasis on filial responsibility. “The proposed law will focus on the responsibility of children in caring for their parents,” he said.

items.title: Shell quarterly net profit jumps 19pct as war fuels oil prices
items.summary: LONDON: British energy giant Shell announced Thursday a 19-per cent increase in net profits for the first quarter as the Middle East war sent oil and gas prices soaring in volatile trading. Profit after tax increased to US$5.69 billion in the January-March period from US$4.78 billion in the first quarter of 2025, Shell said in an earnings statement. Shell's total revenue was stable at around US$70 billion in the first quarter year-on-year but rose from US$66.7 billion in the fourth quarter of 2025.

items.title: Invasive suckerfish choke Jakarta rivers in mounting environmental crisis
items.summary: JAKARTA: Mounds of slimey carcasses pile up on a riverbank in Jakarta where authorities are fighting an uphill battle against a fast-breeding invasive fish flourishing in Java island's heavily polluted waterways. Unlike in the Amazon River where it has natural predators, the sapu-sapu quickly took over in Java, outbreeding indigenous freshwater fish, eating their eggs and outcompeting them for food. "That there are thousands of (sapu-sapu) fish in some of these river bodies, where, you know, the rivers are like dark black, almost smelling like rotten eggs... is completely crazy," clean river campaigner Gary Bencheghib told AFP.

items.title: Bank Negara holds OPR steady at 2.75pct
items.summary: "The MPC will remain vigilant to ongoing developments and assess the balance of risks surrounding the outlook for domestic inflation and growth," it said in a statement. Bank Negara said global growth remained resilient in the first quarter of 2026, supported mainly by sustained domestic demand and continued global tech expansion. "Moving forward, uncertainties surrounding the duration and severity of the Middle East conflict will affect the outlook of domestic growth and inflation.

items.title: SD Guthrie cautious on outlook after Q1 earnings eases to RM560mil
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: SD Guthrie Bhd, one of the world's largest palm oil producers, is cautious on its outlook for the year, after reporting a slightly lower first-quarter net profit on weaker crude palm oil and palm kernel price. Its net profit fell 1.2 per cent to RM560 million in the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 from RM567 million a year ago. The segment was affected by lower realised crude palm oil (CPO) and palm kernel prices, which fell 10 per cent and eight per cent respectively from a year earlier.

items.title: Most EU citizens do not see US as trustworthy partner, study finds
items.summary: BERLIN, (Germany): A majority of citizens in the European Union do not see the United States as a trustworthy partner, according to a study based on surveys in all 27 member states. According to the study, 58 per cent of EU citizens said the US was not a trustworthy partner. In Germany, the figure was even higher at 73 per cent, study author Florian Kommer told dpa.

items.title: Johor among states with highest household income levels since 2022
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI: Household income in Johor has increased since the current state administration took office in 2022, placing the state among the highest in Malaysia in terms of household income levels. State Investment, Trade, Consumer Affairs and Human Resources Committee chairman Lee Ting Han said data from the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) showed that Johor's median household income in 2022 stood at about RM6,879 per month, while average household income was around RM8,517. Based on the latest 2024/2025 data, the average household income in the state has since risen to about RM9,484 per month, while the median household income increased to around RM7,712 per month.

items.title: EU countries, lawmakers clinch provisional deal on watered-down AI rules
items.summary: BRUSSELS: EU countries and European Parliament lawmakers on Thursday agreed to watered-down landmark artificial intelligence rules, including delaying their implementation, in a move which critics say shows Europe caving to Big Tech. "Today's agreement on the AI Act significantly supports our companies by reducing recurring administrative costs," Marilena Raouna, Cyprus's deputy minister for European affairs, said in a statement. Cyprus currently holds the rotating EU Council presidency.

items.title: Zahid: Umno not rejecting Aminuddin outright, but he should not interfere in Negeri Sembilan royal affairs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 7 — Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has clarified that the party is not outright rejecting Negeri Sembilan MB Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun, but instead withholding support over concerns related to alleged interference in Negeri Sembilan’s customary laws. Zahid said the position was not limited to Umno Negeri Sembilan alone, but also reflected the party’s broader stance. This comes after Umno Negeri Sembilan issued a statement expressing support for the unity government while at the same time rejecting Aminuddin.

items.title: No Umno proposal to replace Aminuddin as Negri MB, says Zahid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: There is no proposal from Umno to replace Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun as Negri Sembilan menteri besar, says party president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. Zahid was responding to a question on whether there was a need to replace the Negri Sembilan menteri besar after Umno's 14 assemblymen said they would not reverse their decision to withdraw support for Aminuddin. Previously, Umno and Pakatan Harapan (PH) said they would maintain their cooperation in Negri Sembilan.

items.title: Selangor Domestic Trade Ministry foils LPG misappropriation, seizes 250 cylinders in Kuala Langat estate
items.summary: Its director Muhamad Hanif Asa’ari said the suspects were caught transferring LPG from subsidised 14-kilogramme cylinders into 50-kg industrial cylinders during the Ops Tiris 4.0 raid conducted with the police at about 3 pm yesterday, following a month-long intelligence operation. “The syndicate obtained subsidised LPG supplies from licensed wholesalers in Banting, Tanjung Sepat and Sungai Pelek to avoid detection by the authorities. Muhamad Hanif said the operation prevented an estimated RM750,000 in subsidy leakages.

items.title: Sri Lanka arrests 231 foreigners in latest crackdown on cyberscams
items.summary: COLOMBO: Sri Lanka police have arrested 231 foreign nationals, mostly Chinese and Vietnamese, following raids on suspected cyberscam centres in the capital since the weekend, officials said Thursday. In Sri Lanka, authorities cracked down on two suspected cybserscam centres on Sunday, arresting a total of 157 people. Immigration authorities arrested 135 Chinese men and women in March for allegedly running a similar scam operation.

items.title: Health minister: Malaysia must strengthen clinical research ecosystem to become global contributor
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 7 — Malaysia must move beyond being merely a recipient or a conduit of external innovation by shaping its own unique footprint on the global map of medical discovery, says Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad. He said that, to place Malaysia on the global map of medical discovery, the country must empower its human capital, intensify and expand collaborative networks, and strengthen a sustainable and competitive ecosystem across all aspects of research. Also present were Dr Akhmal Yusof, chief executive officer of Clinical Research Malaysia; Dr Yasuhiro Fujiwara, chief executive of the Pharmaceuticals and Medical Devices Agency; and Dr Timothy Yap, medical oncologist and physician-scientist at the University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Centre.

items.title: Higher shipping costs, longer lead times adversely hit Malaysian manufacturers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Freight and logistics disruptions continue to weigh on Malaysian manufacturers amid Middle East crisis, with companies facing sharply higher shipping costs and longer delivery times. The Federation of Malaysian Manufacturing (FMM) said 87 per cent of survey respondents reported freight costs had increased since Feb 28, while half of them experienced increases of between 20 and 50 per cent above pre-conflict levels. Domestic logistics have also deteriorated, with 49 per cent of respondents reporting higher transport costs within Malaysia.

items.title: MCMC urges Malaysians to vote for nation’s shortlisted projects at WSIS Prizes 2026
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 7 — The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) is calling on Malaysians to back the nation’s technological innovations and inclusive development efforts by voting for Malaysia’s nominated projects at the World Summit on the Information Society (WSIS) Prizes 2026. The shortlisted projects cover key areas including digital governance, online safety, ICT innovation, agricultural technology and community empowerment. The nominated projects are Asean 5G Skills Framework and Capacity Development; Platform Communications & Infrastructure Disaster Recovery (CIDR) Plan; Human-Centered Digital Governance Through MCMC’s Five-Year Plan 2030 and AgriNXT: Oil Palm Chapter (OPC).

items.title: MCMC tightens QoS standards for TV, radio providers
items.summary: CYBERJAYA: The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) has announced revised mandatory quality of service (QoS) standards for content applications service providers (CASPs) to strengthen customer protection and improve complaint resolution. In a statement today, MCMC said the revised standards apply to all CASPs delivering television and radio content nationwide, including free-to-air television, subscription-based television and terrestrial radio stations, involving 34 active providers. "The revised standards go beyond just ensuring services are up and running, placing stronger focus on how well services actually perform for consumers, including faster complaint resolution and more consistent service quality," the commission said.

items.title: Hunger deepening in Gaza as families survive on one meal a day: UN
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The UN on Wednesday warned that hunger remains widespread and severe across the Gaza Strip, with millions struggling to access adequate food despite ongoing humanitarian efforts, Anadolu Ajansi reported. Speaking to reporters, UN spokesman Stephane Dujarric cited data from the Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs (OCHA), which estimates that 2.1 million people in Gaza are now confined to less than half of the territory, limiting access to farmland, food supplies, and essential services. Citing the World Food Programme (WFP), Dujarric said more than 1.6 million people receive food parcels, hot meals, bread, or cash assistance each month.

items.title: Royal institution vital in ensuring integrity in state governance, says Selangor Umno
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Selangor Umno welcomes and supports the statement yesterday by the Selangor Council of the Royal Court regarding the role and responsibilities of the Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, as a Constitutional Ruler. State Umno liaison committee chairman Datuk Megat Zulkarnain Omardin said the statement was important in clarifying to the public that every decree, advice, reprimand and reminder issued by the Sultan of Selangor was in line with the spirit of Constitutional Monarchy and His Royal Highness' role as the state ruler, with priority given to the interests of the people and the stability of the state. "The statement is very important in clarifying to the people that every decree, advice, reprimand and reminder issued by the Sultan of Selangor is in line with the spirit of Constitutional Monarchy and His Royal Highness' role as state ruler, prioritising the interests of the people and the stability of the state," he said in a statement today.

items.title: Online seller charged in JB with cheating after buyer pays RM41,000 for non‑existent Hermes handbag
items.summary: The accused, Christina Tseu Cheau Wei, 36, was alleged to have deceived Teh Ann Na, 53, into transferring RM41,288 to her bank account for a Hermes Mini Lindy handbag at Laguna Heights Condominium on August 26 last year, The Star reported. Deputy Public Prosecutor Nur Fatihah Mohd Nizam sought bail of RM10,000. Magistrate A. Shaarmini fixed bail at RM4,000 with one surety and set June 9 for mention.

items.title: For Taman Segar’s older residents, a simple push to save a playground can mean the world
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 7 — For Taman Segar resident Swee Len, 80, happiness at her age can be found in the simplest pleasure — a daily morning walk at a playground near her house. So when Minister in the Prime Minister Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh announced that a park smaller than half a football pitch is now safe from development, Swee Len breathed a sigh of relief. Almost daily, in the morning, the senior community will have this part to walk,” the octogenarian said.

items.title: High political costs may undermine democracy, warns IDEAS
items.summary: The independent think tank launched two policy papers raising concerns over opaque political financing and the absence of a comprehensive framework governing executive conduct during political transitions, the statement said. The first paper, titled "How Costly are Political Parties? Exploring Political Expenses and Implications for Democracy", found that political financing in Malaysia remains expensive, uneven and lacking transparency, with newer parties and underrepresented groups facing significant financial barriers.

items.title: Assistant warden arrested over teen's alleged sexual assault
items.summary: GUA MUSANG: Police have arrested an assistant warden at a secondary school to assist in investigations into the alleged sexual assault of a 17-year-old male student late last month. Gua Musang police chief Superintendent Sik Choon Foo said the 37-year-old suspect was detained yesterday following a report lodged by the victim. "The student recounted the incident to a teacher, who then accompanied the victim to lodge a police report.

items.title: Malayan Flour Mills expands into prepared food segment
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malayan Flour Mills Bhd (MFM) has expanded into the ready-to-eat (RTE) meals segment as part of its 60th anniversary initiatives, reflecting growing consumer demand for convenient food products. The group launched a new range of microwaveable protein-based meals, marking a move further into prepared food solutions leveraging its existing poultry processing operations. The new RTE products are produced through the group's vertically integrated operations including Dindings Poultry Processing Sdn Bhd in Manjung, Perak.

items.title: Asian currencies rise, stocks hit record high on Iran peace deal hopes
items.summary: HONG KONG: Asian currencies gained, and stocks rallied to a record high on Thursday, buoyed by hopes that the Middle East war would end as Iran reviewed a US peace proposal, sending oil prices lower and fuelling risk plays. The MSCI's gauge of emerging Asian stocks hit a record high and is up 23.4 per cent this year so far. Thai and Singapore stocks rose 0.2 per cent each.

items.title: RM3.95mil contraband cigarettes and alcohol seized in Penang raids
items.summary: PENANG: The Customs Department has dealt a major blow to a smuggling syndicate following two separate raids in Seberang Prai last month, seizing contraband cigarettes and alcoholic beverages worth RM3.95 million, including unpaid duties and taxes. She said a team from the Penang Customs Enforcement Division raided a shop premises in Tasek Gelugor, suspected of being used as a storage facility for smuggled cigarettes. Customs seized 242,640 sticks of cigarettes in the raid, with the total value of the confiscated goods, including duties and taxes, estimated at RM263,233.08.

items.title: Malaysia ready to absorb 850 NUMed students affected by UK policy shift
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is prepared to accommodate 850 Newcastle University Medicine Malaysia (NUMed) students affected by changes to the United Kingdom's medical training policy, allowing them to complete their housemanship locally. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad assured the students that they would not be left stranded despite the policy shift in the UK. Dzulkefly said the Medical Development Division had confirmed there were no objections to the students undergoing training in Malaysia using existing modules, subject to current entry requirements.

items.title: Man charged with grievous hurt after wife miscarries following assault with chair, hose and hanger in Sungai Petani
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 7 — A man was charged in the Sungai Petani Sessions Court today with injuring his wife using several objects in March and April. The accused, Rosmaini Abdul Raof, 43, pleaded not guilty after two charges were read before Judge Najwa Che Mat, Sinar Harian reported. State prosecution director Datuk Khairul Anuar Abdul Halim led the case, while Rosmaini was represented by National Legal Aid Foundation (YBGK) lawyer Ruzanna Abdul Rahim.

items.title: 20km police chase suspect claims trial to 14 charges
items.summary: The offences include obstructing a police officer, reckless driving and committing mischief by damaging multiple vehicles during the incident on April 30. Twelve charges were framed under Section 427 of the Penal Code, while the remaining charges were under Section 186 of the Penal Code and Section 42(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987. At Nadratun's court, Prasaanndh faced five charges, including allegedly obstructing a police corporal from carrying out his duties at Jalan Tanjung Bungah at about 9am on April 30.

items.title: Malaysia's services PPI up 2.1pct in Q1: Statistics Department
items.summary: "The accommodation and F&B service activities index rose 5.7 per cent, compared with 3.1 per cent in the previous quarter, driven by higher restaurant and mobile food service activities index (5.2 per cent) and beverage service activities (3.1 per cent). On education, Mohd Uzir said the index increased 1.9 per cent (Q4 2025: 1.5 per cent), supported by secondary education (1.7 per cent) and higher education (1.5 per cent). "Other indices that registered increases were arts, entertainment and recreation (1.6 per cent), health (1.5 per cent), professional services (1.0 per cent) and real estate activities (0.6 per cent)," he said.

items.title: Johor MB says inclusion of five non-elected assembly seats to widen policy input
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI, May 7 — A proposed amendment to the Johor constitution allowing the appointment of five unelected state assemblymen is aimed at strengthening the legislative institution, Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said today. “With that, the state government sees the need to strengthen the state assembly’s institution by having individuals with expertise and experience in certain fields to assist in the process,” he said when tabling the Bill at the Johor state legislative assembly at Bangunan Sultan Ismail in Kota Iskandar here. The Johor legislative assembly today approved a Bill to amend the state constitution to appoint five unelected assemblymen.

items.title: Malaysia to strengthen elderly care through new legislation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is mulling introducing a new law on parental care aimed at making the care of elderly parents a responsibility of their children. Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the Women, Family and Community Development Ministry has been directed to draft and present the legislation to the cabinet within the next six months. Speaking at the launch of the National Social Policy Action Plan 2026–2030 today, Zahid said the proposed law could draw on examples from countries that have already implemented similar legislation.

items.title: Wasco Greenergy installs one of Malaysia's largest biomass steam systems
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Wasco Greenergy Bhd has installed and commissioned one of Malaysia's largest biomass steam energy systems at a paper manufacturing facility in Kajang, Selangor. The company said the project supports the industry's transition towards more efficient and sustainable energy solutions. Wasco Greenergy said the system uses a cogeneration setup to produce both renewable electricity and steam energy, improving efficiency and operational reliability.

items.title: DBKL steps up flood mitigation plan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) is preparing short, medium and long-term measures to reduce flash floods in high-risk areas following heavy rain in several parts of the city yesterday. Mayor Datuk Seri Fadlun Mak Ujud said heavy rain in several areas, especially Segambut and Cheras, caused rapid surface runoff from higher ground, with drains along roads and in low-lying areas unable to cope with the volume of water during the downpour. "During the heavy rain, surface runoff was very fast, especially along roads.

items.title: News accuracy must remain priority despite pressure to publish fast, says deputy communications minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 7 — Accuracy in news reporting must remain a priority despite mounting pressure on newsrooms to publish stories quickly in the social media era, Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching said today. She said the rapid pace of information consumption on digital platforms had significantly changed newsroom operations, with media organisations racing against time to maximise audience reach online. “I was told by our friends in the media industry that sometimes, if you are late by even 50 minutes, 10 minutes or even 5 minutes, the reach you can get on social media can be totally different.

items.title: Malaysia, India strengthen cooperation on biodiversity conservation, rare earths industry and sustainability
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 7 — Malaysia and India are committed to strengthening bilateral cooperation in biodiversity conservation, rare earth elements (REE), and green technology through various strategic initiatives discussed during a courtesy call by the Indian delegation to the Ministry of Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability (NRES). The Indian delegation, led by India’s High Commissioner to Malaysia B. N. Reddy, was hosted by NRES deputy minister Syed Ibrahim Syed Noh. In the natural resources sector, NRES said Malaysia had also expressed interest in exploring strategic collaboration in the REE industry with Indian Rare Earths Limited (IREL).

items.title: Hawana Media Forum seen as timely platform to bolster media integrity and resilience, says Teo Nie Ching
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 7 — The National Journalists’ Day (Hawana) Media Forum is a timely platform for media practitioners to exchange ideas and develop practical solutions to ensure the industry remains relevant and resilient amid rapid transformation. This balance is critical as we navigate the evolving media landscape,” she said. She said Phase 1 was successfully completed, with 34 organisations receiving RM12.38 million.

items.title: Technical glitch disrupts subsidised fuel quotas for SKDS, SKPS fleet card holders, to be restored May 9–11, says Domestic Trade Ministry
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 7 — Technical issues have caused some fleet card holders under the Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS) and Subsidised Petrol Control System (SKPS) to be unable to receive their subsidised fuel quota starting May 1. It said that the issue affects certain vehicle owners who renewed their Motor Vehicle Licence (LKM), registered new vehicles or carried out vehicle ownership transfers between April 1 and 28. The ministry said affected SKDS and SKPS fleet card holders may submit applications for review and updating at https://forms.gle/hQy1ZQZ2NZoBFJgeA no later than 5 pm tomorrow.

items.title: MOHE says SPM holders’ admission results for 2026/2027 session out May 22
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 7 — The outcome of applications for admission into public universities (UA), public higher education institutions (IPTA) and the MARA Higher Education Division (MARA BPT) for Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia holders will be announced on May 22, said the Ministry of Higher Education (MOHE). “Acceptance of offers is open from May 23 to 31, 2026, via the websites of the UA, IPTA and MARA BPT, while official offer letters will be issued to successful applicants beginning May 23 after candidates confirm acceptance within the stipulated period,” it said. The ministry said only unsuccessful applicants or those offered certificate-level programmes may submit appeals through UPU Online within 10 days, from 12 noon on May 22 until 5pm on May 31.

items.title: Johor MB tables Bill to appoint five unelected assemblymen
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI, May 7 — Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi today tabled a Bill to amend the state constitution to allow the appointment of five unelected state assemblymen. Onn Hafiz said the amendment would increase the number of Johor assemblymen from 56 to 61, adding that the five additional assemblymen would require the consent of the Ruler before being appointed. “These assemblymen are not appointed to complement the existing composition of assemblymen in Johor.

items.title: Kedah Sultan’s birthday moved from June 21 to July 5, public holiday changed
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, May 7 — The official birthday celebration of the Sultan of Kedah Al Aminul Karim Sultan Sallehuddin Sultan Badlishah has been moved from June 21 to July 5 this year. The change in date was announced through an official state government letter signed by deputy state secretary (Management) Muhammad Mahazi Ibrahim, dated May 6. The letter was distributed to heads of state and federal departments, heads of state and federal statutory bodies, as well as heads of local authorities for their attention.

items.title: Teo Nie Ching: 464 false content pieces tied to supply crisis identified in April
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 7 — A total of 464 pieces of false content related to the global supply crisis were identified between March 29 and April 30, said Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching. She said 312 of the items had been successfully taken down. Teo said the rapid spread of misinformation, amplified by artificial intelligence (AI), posed a significant challenge to public trust.

items.title: Four men face murder charge over fatal machete attack on 70‑year‑old in Hilir Perak
items.summary: TELUK INTAN, May 7 — Four men were charged today in the Magistrate’s Court here with the murder of an elderly man using a machete in April. The accused, S. R. C. Parkash Rao, M. V. Aligeswaran, T. Tiagesan, and K. Shanker, all aged 25, merely nodded in understanding after the charge was read out before Magistrate Naidatul Athirah Azman. 110, Lorong Emas L2/1, Pekan Baru, Hilir Perak, between 6.00 am and 6.30 am on April 17.

items.title: Perak MB: Economic growth statistics means little if people do not feel the benefits
items.summary: IPOH, May 7 — Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad today said government achievements should not be measured by economic figures alone, but by whether people feel the benefits in their daily lives. Saarani said that while growth and investment numbers remain important, the real test of any policy is whether it improves the realities faced by the public. “Today, we often talk about growth figures, investment figures and economic achievement figures.

items.title: Johor MB quashes rumours of state assembly dissolution today
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI, May 7 — Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi has quashed rumours that the state assembly will be dissolved today, paving the way for a snap state election. “I heard there were rumours that the PRN (state election) will soon be called together with various speculation on the dissolution of the state assembly,” he said in his winding-up speech at the Johor state legislative assembly at Bangunan Sultan Ismail in Kota Iskandar here today. The present state legislative assembly began its term on April 21, 2022, following the state election on March 12, 2022.

items.title: Police: 11-year-old dies after motorcycle collision with another underage rider
items.summary: SIK, May 7 — An 11-year-old boy has reportedly died from severe head injuries yesterday evening after his motorcycle crashed into another underage rider’s vehicle along Jalan Kampung Betong. The younger boy was reportedly rushed to Sik Hospital but was pronounced dead at 8.45pm while receiving treatment, whereas the 13-year-old sustained only minor injuries. Initial police investigations indicate the crash happened as both boys were traveling toward Kampung Betong.

items.title: Immigration Dept arrests two Indonesians over fake security stamp syndicate in KL raids
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 5 — The Immigration Department has arrested two Indonesian men believed to be behind a fake Immigration security stamps syndicate during operations conducted in Taman Kepong Indah and Taman Dagang, Ampang here yesterday. Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said investigations revealed that the two men, identified as ‘Padi’ and ‘Amir’, both 52, did not possess valid passes or travel documents. He said the operations led to the seizure of 20 fake Immigration security stamps, 16 copies of Indonesian passports, four copies of Indonesian travel permits, two mobile phones, a laptop, a printer, a scanner, and two vehicles used by the syndicate.

items.title: Sarawak to conduct cloud seeding over key dams to boost water supply
items.summary: KUCHING, May 5 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) will conduct cloud seeding operations in Sarawak on May 11 and 12 to induce rainfall over key dam catchment areas. MetMalaysia, in a statement today, said the operations would focus on the Bakun, Batang Ai and Murum dam catchment areas, which serve as important sources of water supply and hydroelectric power in Sarawak. The department said the operations would involve strategic cooperation between various agencies, including the National Disaster Management Agency (Nadma) and the Royal Malaysian Air Force (RMAF).

items.title: Court grants Malaysian Bar green light to challenge DNAA in Zahid’s Yayasan Akalbudi case
items.summary: Datuk Faizah Jamaludin, who chaired a three-judge panel at the Court of Appeal, said the panel unanimously decided that the Malaysian Bar’s court challenge could proceed to be heard by the High Court. The Malaysian Bar had on December 2, 2023 filed its court challenge via a judicial review application, but the High Court on June 27, 2024 decided not to grant leave for the case to be heard. The High Court will now hear the Malaysian Bar’s challenge against the AG’s decision to get a DNAA in Zahid’s case, unless the AG decides to file an appeal on today’s decision.

items.title: Melaka local councils to give small traders one-month rent waiver, 30 per cent discount starting July 1
items.summary: MELAKA, May 5 — The one-month business premises rent waiver initiative introduced by the Melaka state government, effective July 1, is expected to provide significant relief to small-scale traders across the state. State Senior Executive Councillor for Housing, Local Government, Drainage, Climate Change and Disaster Management, Datuk Rais Yasin, said this measure applies to all premises managed by local authorities (PBT) throughout Melaka. He said the initiative will cost the state government RM2.6 million.

items.title: National Art Gallery awaits 1MDB-linked paintings with ‘meticulous preparations’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 5 — The National Art Gallery is prepared to receive four international masterpieces acquired with 1MDB funds, which have now been repatriated to Malaysia. In a statement today, the National Art Gallery informed that the handover process for the four artworks, scheduled to be received next week in phases, will be carried out meticulously to ensure compliance with every aspect of handling. “Meticulous preparations also involve storage and conservation facilities, including a specialised conservation room equipped with stable temperature and humidity control systems.

items.title: Five-year jail term for former policeman who blackmailed man with intimate videos
items.summary: A three-member bench of Court of Appeal judge Datuk Azman Abdullah has unanimously dismissed Mohd Aman Mustapa’s final challenge to his conviction. The 36-year-old had sought to overturn the Taiping High Court ruling on June 5, 2025, that jailed him for five years. Noorin, reading the decision today, said the court was satisfied that the High Court judge had thoroughly examined all evidence, including trial notes, witness statements and recordings, and found no misdirection warranting the Court of Appeal’s intervention.

items.title: Pakatan rallies grassroots in Seremban rally after Negeri Sembilan political strain
items.summary: SEREMBAN, May 7 — As the Pakatan Harapan–Barisan Nasional (PH-BN) coalition government in Negeri Sembilan regains its footing, senior Pakatan leaders have stepped up efforts to rally grassroots support following recent political fallout that highlighted strains within the fragile alliance. Even after Negeri Sembilan Umno indicated that all its 14 state lawmakers would continue to work with the ruling government in the state, Aminuddin expressed his commitment to administering the state until the time comes, despite the former confirming it had withdrawn support for him. My colleagues here, especially in the state government, including my exco members, state assemblyman and party leadership, must all continue to work to ensure the state is well governed.

items.title: ‘Stay prepared’: Pakatan leaders say N. Sembilan snap election could come anytime
items.summary: SEREMBAN, May 7 — After weeks of political uncertainty in Negeri Sembilan, the Pakatan Harapan top leadership has urged supporters and residents to brace for a possible snap election, as its relationship with the state’s Umno chapter remains strained. “I want the people of Negeri Sembilan to prepare for an election,” Aminuddin said during a rally here last night. In its latest announcement, Negeri Sembilan Umno secretary Datuk Mustapha Nagoor said it respects the party central leadership’s decision to continue working together with the unity government in Negeri Sembilan.

items.title: ‘No one gets rich in politics unless he’s a crook’: Selangor Sultan shares biting quotes on corruption
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 7 — The Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, has shared a poignant reminder on the perils of political corruption, quoting former United States President Harry S. Truman to highlight the ethical boundaries of public service. In a post shared by the Selangor Royal Office on Facebook today, the Sultan expressed that he was “struck” by a specific quote attributed to the 33rd US President and wished to share its wisdom with the people. The Sultan of Selangor has long been a vocal advocate for integrity and good governance.
